cards.back: A combination of physical RAM and hard disk storage that appears as a single, contiguous block of memory to the operating system
Page-based virtual memory and Segmented virtual memory
A type of virtual memory where the physical RAM is divided into fixed-size blocks called pages, and the operating system uses a page table to manage memory
A type of virtual memory where the physical RAM is divided into segments, and each segment is assigned a unique memory address
A memory address that is unique to a process, and is used to access virtual memory, regardless of the physical location of the memory
